evidence_note: Most of the window is before stated COD (2023-07-01) with zero output - clearly pre-commissioning. COD from dataset.

potential_reason: First operating winter after July 2023 COD; q50r 0.548, q90r 0.721 - a mild shortfall. Consistent with final commissioning ramp-up / staged turbine handover, or early intermittent curtailment in constrained South Lanarkshire.
source_name: Modo Energy - Constraints in Great Britain: Scottish wind curtailment
evidence_note: Within ~5 months of COD; mild shortfall could be ramp-up or early curtailment. No farm-specific source.

potential_reason: Summer 2024 shortfall to ~0% of expected (q50r 0.000) with q90r 0.379. Same window as the parent Kype Muir flag (11459), suggesting a site-wide cause - heavy curtailment behind the SW Scotland constraint or a coincident outage. Recommend cross-checking with the main Kype Muir flag.
source_name: Energy Voice - Scottish wind curtailment costs hit £343m
evidence_note: Co-occurs with parent-site flag 11459 (same Jul-Aug 2024 window) - points to a common site-wide driver (curtailment or outage). q50=0 warrants availability check. No farm-specific source.

potential_reason: Limekiln (106 MW, Caithness, Boralex) was declared operational on 1 April 2025 (COD 2025-04-01). This Oct-Dec 2024 period precedes COD entirely, with q50r and q90r both 0.000 (no output) - turbines were still being installed/energised. Clear pre-COD commissioning / data artifact.
source_name: Boralex - Limekiln Wind Farm now operational (1 April 2025)
evidence_note: Verified operational 1 April 2025; the flagged Oct-Dec 2024 period is entirely pre-COD with zero output - not a performance issue.

potential_reason: Lochluichart (69 MW, Ross-shire, Highlands, EDF Renewables) has COD 2014-06-01 for the combined Phase 1+2. This flag starts Nov 2013 - ~7 months before COD - and runs ~22 months at ~19% of expected (q90 0.481). The pre-COD start plus a multi-year span on a combined 1+2 asset suggests the early portion is commissioning/pre-operational and the later portion mixes genuine northern-Scotland curtailment with a likely capacity/curve mismatch from Phase 2 timing.
source_name: Modo Energy - Constraints in Great Britain: Scottish wind curtailment
evidence_note: Flag begins before COD and spans nearly 2 years across a combined-phase asset - the early part is pre-commissioning and the modelled capacity likely overstates the operating fleet (false-positive component); residual curtailment in the Highlands also plausible. No farm-specific source.
